codeReview.summary: The worker successfully replaced 12 hardcoded inline style values with CSS variables across 11 component files. Added 9 new CSS variables to theme.css for line heights, touch targets, border radius, and label width. All type-check, lint, build, and tests pass. One minor documentation discrepancy was found where the handoff claimed to use --line-height-loose (1.6) in SkillMarkdown.tsx but actually used --line-height-relaxed (1.5).

issues.description: Handoff documentation claims 'lineHeight 1.6 → var(--line-height-loose)' but actual code uses var(--line-height-relaxed) which is 1.5. The original value was 1.6, so --line-height-loose would be the correct replacement. Minor visual difference, not blocking.
Same issue as above - uses var(--line-height-relaxed) instead of var(--line-height-loose) for ordered lists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The typo.ts configuration file (lib/config/typo.ts) still contains hardcoded line-height values (1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6) rather than CSS variables. While the feature focused on inline styles in components, the typo presets are used across the codebase and could benefit from CSS variable references for consistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/config/typo.ts defines 12 style presets with hardcoded lineHeight values that could reference --line-height-tight through --line-height-loose

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-styling-worker skill provides clear guidance but doesn't explicitly mention typography configuration files like typo.ts as a potential target for CSS variable migration. This led to inconsistency where component inline styles were updated but the centralized typography presets were not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature replaced inline styles in components but left hardcoded values in lib/config/typo.ts untouched

summary: Feature passed review. Worker successfully replaced hardcoded inline style values with CSS variables, fulfilling VAL-STYLE-001 (no hardcoded colors) and VAL-STYLE-002 (inline styles minimized). Two non-blocking issues found: a documentation/implementation discrepancy in SkillMarkdown.tsx where --line-height-relaxed (1.5) was used instead of --line-height-loose (1.6), and an opportunity to extend CSS variable usage to the typo.ts configuration file for complete consistency.
